title: teucrium orientale subsp. glabrescens
intro: Teucrium orientale subsp. glabrescens, also known as the Oriental Germander, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Teucrium orientale, native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Oriental Germander include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve cognitive function, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Oriental Germander can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin conditions.
